steps.title: Recall the hemisphere volume formula
steps.content: A hemisphere is half of a sphere. The volume of a sphere is $V_{sphere} = \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$, so the volume of a hemisphere is half of that:

steps.title: Calculate the cube of the radius
Substitute and compute the volume
steps.content:
$$V = \frac{2}{3}\pi(571,787)$$
Using $\pi \approx 3.14159$:
$$V \approx \frac{2}{3}(3.14159)(571,787) \approx 2.0944 \times 571,787 \approx 1,197,517$$
This is closest to 1,197,500. That matches choice D — D is correct.
